name: Data Lakehouse
description: Multi-source data ingestion and transformation platform with a medallion architecture (bronze/silver/gold). Handles batch and micro-batch ingestion, SQL-based transformation via dbt, and serves analytical queries to BI tools or downstream ML pipelines.

defaultStack.choice: Apache Airflow (local, Astro CLI for dev)
defaultStack.rationale: DAG-based retry logic, sensor operators for upstream dependencies, built-in SLA monitoring. Prefer over Prefect for teams already on Airflow.
ACID transactions, time travel, and schema enforcement without a Spark cluster. Works with DuckDB for local query. Upgrade path: Databricks Delta or Snowflake.
SQL-first, version-controlled transformations. Models map cleanly to bronze→silver→gold layers. Lineage and docs are first-class.
In-process OLAP on Delta/Parquet files. Zero infra, fast aggregations on GB-scale data. Upgrade path: Trino or Spark for TB-scale.
Lightweight for REST API ingestion. Pydantic catches schema drift at ingest time before it reaches the bronze layer.
Cursor-based incremental extraction avoids full-table scans. Watermark stored in SQLite control table.
Expectation suites run post-ingestion on silver layer. Failures block gold layer promotion and trigger Airflow alerts.

phaseHints: Phase 1 — Bronze ingestion: Airflow DAGs per source, raw landing in Delta bronze layer, Pydantic schema validation, watermark tracking in SQLite control table
Phase 2 — Silver transformation: dbt models for cleaning, deduplication, type casting, and join enrichment; Great Expectations suite on silver output
Phase 3 — Gold serving layer: dbt models for business aggregates, DuckDB views for BI consumption, schema versioning and backwards-compatibility rules
Phase 4 — Observability & SLA: Airflow SLA miss alerts, row count anomaly detection, freshness checks, lineage documentation via dbt docs

architectureDecisions.rationale: Immutable bronze preserves audit trail. Silver isolates cleaning logic from business logic. Gold is the only layer BI tools query.

architectureDecisions.choice: Incremental by default; full refresh only on schema change or backfill
architectureDecisions.rationale: Full refreshes on large tables block downstream SLAs. Watermark-based incremental keeps DAG runtime under 10 minutes at GB scale.

architectureDecisions.choice: claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 via Batch API for unstructured field classification only
architectureDecisions.rationale: Use LLMs only for fields that cannot be parsed with SQL (free-text categories, address normalization). Keep LLM calls out of the hot path.

costModelHint: Infrastructure cost near-zero for local dev (DuckDB, SQLite, dbt). Haiku Batch if LLM classification is in scope: ~$0.04/MTok input. Primary cost is Airflow worker compute if cloud-hosted. Target: full pipeline run under $0.10/day at GB scale.
